options.text: I need a workout plan for building muscle and cardio, 3 days a week at home with dumbbells and pull-up bar, but I have a shoulder injury so keep that in mind when suggesting exercises.

Can you create a 3-day weekly workout routine for me? I want to build upper body strength and improve cardio. I have dumbbells and a pull-up bar at home, and each workout should be around 45 minutes. I have a previous shoulder injury on my left side that I need to be careful with.

Please create a structured 3-day weekly workout routine with the following specifications:

GOALS:
- Build upper body strength
- Improve cardiovascular fitness

CONSTRAINTS:
- 3 days per week maximum
- 45 minutes per session
- Home gym only

AVAILABLE EQUIPMENT:
- Dumbbells (various weights)
- Pull-up bar

MEDICAL CONSIDERATIONS:
- Previous shoulder injury on left side - please modify or avoid exercises that could aggravate this

Please organize the routine by day and include warm-up/cool-down recommendations.

responses.response_weak: Here's a 3-day workout plan you can do at home with dumbbells and a pull-up bar. Day 1 focuses on upper body push movements: dumbbell bench press, overhead press, push-ups, and tricep extensions. Day 2 is cardio: do 30 minutes of jumping jacks, burpees, mountain climbers, and high knees in a circuit. Day 3 is upper body pull: pull-ups, dumbbell rows, bicep curls, and shrugs. For your shoulder injury, just go lighter on overhead movements and stop if anything hurts. Make sure to stretch before and after each workout.
responses.response_medium:
Here's a 3-day routine designed around your goals and equipment, keeping your left shoulder in mind.

Day 1 -- Upper Body Strength: Start with 5 minutes of arm circles and light stretching. Then do dumbbell floor press (3x10), supported single-arm rows (3x10 each side), neutral-grip pull-ups (3x6-8), hammer curls (3x12), and tricep kickbacks (3x12). Use lighter weight on your left side for pressing movements.

Day 2 -- Cardio Circuit: Warm up with 3 minutes of marching in place. Then cycle through dumbbell thrusters (light weight), mountain climbers, goblet squats, and high knees -- 40 seconds on, 20 seconds rest, for 4 rounds. Cool down with 5 minutes of walking and stretching.

Day 3 -- Full Body: Warm up 5 minutes. Do goblet squats (3x12), dumbbell Romanian deadlifts (3x10), incline push-ups (3x10), dumbbell reverse lunges (3x10 each), and plank holds (3x30 seconds). Avoid any overhead pressing to protect your shoulder.

This should fit within 45 minutes per session. Increase weight gradually and pay attention to how your left shoulder responds.
responses.response_strong:
## 3-Day Home Workout Routine

---

### Day 1: Upper Body Strength (Push Focus)

**Warm-Up (5 minutes)**
- Arm circles: 30 seconds each direction
- Wall slides: 10 reps (gentle shoulder activation)
- Light dumbbell halos: 8 reps each direction (skip if left shoulder objects)

**Main Workout (35 minutes)**
| Exercise | Sets x Reps | Shoulder Notes |
|----------|-------------|----------------|
| Dumbbell floor press | 3 x 10 | Safer than bench -- limits range of motion |
| Neutral-grip pull-ups | 3 x 6-8 | Neutral grip reduces shoulder strain |
| Single-arm dumbbell row (supported) | 3 x 10 each | Use lighter weight on left side |
| Hammer curls | 3 x 12 | Neutral grip is shoulder-friendly |
| Tricep kickbacks | 3 x 12 | No shoulder involvement |
| Farmer's walks | 2 x 30 sec | Builds grip and core without shoulder stress |

**Cool-Down (5 minutes)**
- Chest doorway stretch: 30 seconds each side
- Cross-body shoulder stretch: 30 seconds each side
- Tricep overhead stretch: 20 seconds each side

---

### Day 2: Cardio + Core

**Warm-Up (5 minutes)**
- Marching in place: 2 minutes
- Bodyweight squats: 10 reps
- Arm swings: 30 seconds

**Main Workout (35 minutes)** -- Circuit Format, 4 Rounds
- Goblet squats (moderate dumbbell): 40 seconds
- Mountain climbers: 40 seconds
- Dumbbell step-ups (alternating legs): 40 seconds
- High knees: 40 seconds
- Dead bugs: 40 seconds
- Plank hold: 30 seconds
- **Rest: 90 seconds between rounds**

**Cool-Down (5 minutes)**
- Walking in place: 2 minutes
- Seated forward fold: 30 seconds
- Deep breathing: 1 minute

---

### Day 3: Functional Strength (Pull + Lower Body)

**Warm-Up (5 minutes)**
- Leg swings: 10 each direction
- Hip circles: 10 each direction
- Light dumbbell deadlifts: 8 reps

**Main Workout (35 minutes)**
| Exercise | Sets x Reps | Notes |
|----------|-------------|-------|
| Dumbbell Romanian deadlifts | 3 x 10 | Builds posterior chain |
| Goblet squats | 3 x 12 | Front-loaded for core engagement |
| Inverted rows (pull-up bar, low position) | 3 x 8-10 | Easier on shoulders than full pull-ups |
| Dumbbell reverse lunges | 3 x 10 each leg | No shoulder load |
| Modified push-ups (incline on counter) | 3 x 10-12 | Reduced shoulder stress vs. floor push-ups |
| Pallof press (dumbbell in both hands) | 3 x 8 each side | Anti-rotation core work |

**Cool-Down (5 minutes)**
- Pigeon stretch: 30 seconds each side
- Quad stretch: 30 seconds each side
- Full-body child's pose: 1 minute

---

## Left Shoulder Injury Protocol
- **Avoid entirely:** Overhead press, behind-the-neck movements, wide-grip pull-ups
- **Modify:** Use neutral grip whenever possible, reduce range of motion on pressing
- **Monitor:** If sharp pain occurs during any exercise, stop immediately and substitute
- **Progress:** Once left shoulder feels stable for 2+ weeks, gradually introduce wider grips and fuller range of motion

feedback.what_happened: The weak prompt bunched everything together, so the AI gave generic advice that could actually be harmful — recommending overhead press despite the shoulder injury and vague guidance like 'just go lighter.' The medium prompt separated the information better, so the AI provided more thoughtful exercise modifications and timing, but still missed key details like specific warm-ups and injury protocols. The effective prompt organized every piece of information into clear categories, so the AI could create a comprehensive plan with detailed modifications, safety protocols, and structured progressions that actually protect the injured shoulder.
feedback.principle: When you organize your information clearly with headers and categories, AI can process each piece properly and give you thorough, safe advice instead of generic suggestions.
feedback.tip: Next time you need help with something that has multiple requirements or constraints, use headers or bullet points to separate different types of information (like GOALS, CONSTRAINTS, EQUIPMENT). This helps AI tools give you more complete and safer recommendations.
